title: session-start.py's host.has_statusline gate is asserted as a flag value but never exercised through main()'s actual skip behavior
evidence: session-start.py:556-557 gates `heal_statusline_wiring(plugin)` on `if host.has_statusline:` inside main() (host = hostapi.load_host() at line 541) — this is the only call site of that capability flag. Two test units touch the pieces but neither exercises the gate itself: (1) test_codex_adapter.py:118-125 (test_name_and_capabilities) only asserts `self.assertFalse(self.host.has_statusline)` on the bare CodexHost instance — it never calls session-start.py's main(). (2) plugins/ca/hooks/tests/test_session_start.py's TestMainHealsBeforeDormantGate (read in full, lines 230-260+) calls `heal_statusline_wiring` directly and separately verifies main() calls it BEFORE the dormant gate, but always against the Claude host loaded from plugins/ca/hooks/_host.py (has_statusline=True) — grep of that file confirms no fixture stands up a Codex-shaped host (has_statusline=False) and asserts main() SKIPS the heal call. No test in the repo drives session-start.py's main() end-to-end under CodexHost.
impact: The one production call site that actually depends on has_statusline being correct (skipping a statusline self-heal that would be a no-op-at-best, or write globally to ~/.claude/settings.json for a host with no statusline surface, at worst) is unverified; a refactor that drops or inverts the `if host.has_statusline:` gate would pass the whole suite.
recommendation: Add a subprocess or in-process test that runs plugins/ca-codex/hooks/session-start.py's main() (or the module loaded via the CODEX_HOOKS path, mirroring TestPruneEntryHostGate's pattern in test_codex_adapter.py) against a fixture ~/.claude/settings.json carrying a stale ca-owned statusLine pin, and asserts the pin is left UNCHANGED (heal skipped) — mirroring the existing Claude-side test_stale_ours_pin_is_healed but for the Codex host.
acceptance_criteria: A test drives session-start.py main() under a CodexHost (has_statusline=False) and asserts heal_statusline_wiring is not invoked / settings.json is untouched
An equivalent Claude-host test continues to assert the heal DOES run (regression guard for the existing behavior)
